onboarding.levelTitle: How much did you use to drink?
onboarding.levelHint: Optional — helps us personalize your messages

relapse.title: You are not alone
relapse.subtitle: Starting over is always possible. No judgment.
relapse.farewell: You had {{count}} days of sobriety. That is a victory no one can erase. Your new start begins now.

settings.title: Settings
settings.name: Name
settings.consentBody: Share anonymous usage data (no name, no email, no personal content) so we can improve the app. You can turn this off anytime.
settings.privacy: Your data stays on your device. Nothing is sent without your explicit consent.

h24.label: 24 hours
h24.title: Alcohol-free body
h24.body: Alcohol is fully eliminated from your blood. Rehydration improves digestion, brain function and energy. Your liver goes back to regulating blood sugar.

w1.label: 1 week
w1.title: Real sleep
w1.body: Significant improvement in sleep quality (alcohol disrupts REM). Fewer headaches and better focus.

w2.label: 2 weeks
w2.title: Calmer digestion
w2.body: Stomach acid production normalizes and acid reflux decreases. Noticeable gains in concentration begin.

w3.label: 3 weeks
w3.title: Lighter liver
w3.body: Reduction of liver fat accumulated from alcohol use.

m1.label: 1 month
m1.title: Blood pressure & wellbeing
m1.body: Blood pressure drops (comparable to medication). Average 1.5% weight loss. Better insulin response and mental wellbeing.

m3.label: 3 months
m3.title: Heart and brain
m3.body: Cardiac function keeps recovering. Cortical thickness begins to recover.

m6.label: 6 months
m6.title: Deep recovery
m6.body: Heart function continues improving. Cognitive gains consolidate.

y1.label: 1 year
y1.title: A new chapter
y1.body: Alcohol-related cancer risk drops by 4–9%. Significant recovery of affected organs. You earned this moment.

tl.sources: Sources: CISA, The Conversation/ScienceAlert, Canadian Medical Association Journal, Mehta et al.

disclaimer.short: Claru is a support tool and does not replace medical or psychological treatment.
disclaimer.sos: ⚕️ Important: in severe dependence, stopping alcohol abruptly can cause withdrawal syndrome (tremors, heavy sweating, confusion, seizures). If you feel these symptoms, seek medical care immediately.

privacy.title: Privacy & Notices
privacy.updated: Draft v1.0 — July 2026 — under review by a privacy professional
privacy.s1b: Everything you record in Claru (name, dates, check-ins, fresh starts) is stored only on your device, in your browser's local storage. We have no server: we cannot see, receive or access your data. If you clear your browser data or switch devices, your history cannot be recovered — cross-device sync will arrive in a future version, with an account.
privacy.s2b: If — and only if — you enable sharing in Settings, we send anonymous usage events (e.g. 'check-in done') to PostHog so we can learn what to improve. These events never include your name, email or any personal content. You can turn it off anytime, which stops sending immediately. Legal basis: consent (LGPD, Art. 11).
privacy.s3b: At any time you can: withdraw consent (Settings), and delete all your data by clearing this site's browsing data — since everything is local, deletion is immediate and complete.
privacy.s4b: Claru is a motivational support and education tool, and does not replace diagnosis, medical treatment or psychological care. In severe dependence, stopping alcohol abruptly can cause serious withdrawal syndrome — seek medical guidance before quitting. In crisis in Brazil, call CVV: 188 (24h, free).
